content.problemStatement:
Given an undirected connected tree with NNN nodes, numbered from 111 to NNN, and rooted at node 111, along with an integer target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum. 

Check if there exists a path from root to any leaf whose sum is equal to target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum. Path sum is the addition of the values of nodes which are in that particular path.

For example, if target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum is 9, then in the following tree, there is a path 1−>2−>61 -> 2 -> 61−>2−>6 whose sum is 9.

Function DeclarationFunction Namesolutionsolutionsolution – This function determines whether there exists a root-to-leaf path in the given tree such that the sum of node values along that path equals target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum.

Parameters

nnn: The number of nodes in the tree (nodes are numbered from 1 to n).




target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum: The required sum of a valid root-to-leaf path.




adjadjadj: An adjacency list representing the tree.



adj[i]adj[i]adj[i] contains the list of nodes directly connected to node iii.
The tree is undirected and rooted at node 1.


Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if there exists at least one path from the root (node 1) to any leaf such that the sum of node values in that path equals target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
Constraints
1≤N≤1000001 \leq N \leq 1000001≤N≤100000
1≤ui,vi≤N1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N1≤ui​,vi​≤N
1≤target_sum≤N(N+1)21 \leq target\_sum \leq \frac{N(N+1)}{2}1≤target_sum≤2N(N+1)​

Input Format

The first line of the input contains two space separated integer NNN and target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum — the number of vertices and the target sum which needs to be checked.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting a bidirectional edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.


Output Format
Output YESYESYES if tree has root to leaf path sum equal to given target_sumtarget\_sumtarget_sum or NONONO otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and target_sum. The next N-1 lines each contain two integers u and v denoting an undirected edge between nodes u and v. The tree is rooted at node 1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if there exists a root‑to‑leaf path whose node values sum to target_sum, otherwise print "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: No root‑to‑leaf path adds up to 4.
Path 1->2->3 (note node 3 is a leaf) sums to 1+2+3=6.

solution.approach: The value of each node equals its index. Perform an iterative DFS/BFS from the root (node 1) while keeping the accumulated sum along the current path. When a leaf (node with no children except its parent) is reached, compare the accumulated sum with target_sum. If any leaf matches, answer is YES; otherwise NO. The traversal visits each edge once, giving O(N) time per test case and O(N) memory for the adjacency list and stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for adjacency lists and the explicit stack.
